tldr: Move from OpenLIT's OTel-native LLM observability to Sensu Go's event-driven monitoring pipeline for hybrid infrastructure.
why_switch: Need event-driven monitoring across hybrid cloud and on-prem rather than LLM-focused tracing
Want agent-based check execution and pipeline-as-code over OTel SDK instrumentation
Prefer a longer-tenured project (since 2017) with Sumo Logic backing
Standardize infra and app monitoring on one tool rather than splitting GenAI observability separately
Reduce reliance on OTel collector pipelines tailored to LLM workloads
what_to_export: Dashboards and saved views from the OpenLIT UI
Instrumentation snippets currently embedded in app code
Historical traces and metrics (if retention required)
Cost and token-usage reports for archival
export_method: Snapshot the self-hosted OpenLIT database (ClickHouse-backed in typical deployments — verify with your install) and export dashboards/configs from the OpenLIT UI or filesystem. For traces, keep the existing OTel collector running long enough to dual-write or archive a window of data to object storage. Consult OpenLIT's official docs for the supported export commands at your version.
import_method: Stand up Sensu Go backend and agents per official docs, then translate monitoring intent into Sensu checks, handlers, and pipelines as YAML/JSON resources applied via sensuctl. Configure runtime assets for portable check execution and wire handlers to your existing notification channels. Any LLM-specific telemetry that Sensu does not natively model should be emitted via custom checks or kept in a parallel OTel pipeline.

concept_mapping.note: Sensu is event-driven, not span-based — model meaningful signals as check results
Replace in-app SDK calls with agent-side checks or pushed events
Sensu Go focuses on pipeline; visualization is typically delegated
Sensu uses handlers triggered by event filters
Express transformations and routing as Sensu pipeline resources
No native LLM model in Sensu — emit as custom metrics

gotchas: Sensu Go is not LLM-aware — GenAI-specific dashboards and evals will need to be rebuilt or kept elsewhere
Trace-level detail does not map cleanly to Sensu's event model; expect lossy translation
Release cadence on Sensu Go is slower (release_frequency_days ~201) — plan upgrade windows accordingly
Self-hosting both systems during migration doubles infra cost temporarily
Retention and storage layout differ; budget time for capacity sizing on Sensu
Confirm licensing (MIT core vs commercial features) before relying on enterprise capabilities

email.subject: Migration plan: OpenLIT to Sensu Go
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move our observability stack from OpenLIT to Sensu Go. OpenLIT served us well for LLM-specific tracing, but we need event-driven monitoring across our hybrid infrastructure, and Sensu's pipeline-as-code model fits better.

Plan: export dashboards, alert rules, and OTel configs from OpenLIT; stand up Sensu Go backend + agents; translate checks, handlers, and pipelines as code; keep a parallel OTel collector for any LLM-specific telemetry Sensu does not natively model. Estimated effort 1-3 weeks.

Main risks: lossy mapping from spans to events, and rebuilding GenAI-specific views outside Sensu. I will consult official docs for both products before each step.

Thoughts?
